title: Beyond CLEAN and MVP: Architecting an Offline-first Reactive Data Layer in Android
gist: A Google senior engineer introduces Reactive Data Layer Architecture (RDLA), a mobile-first Android pattern that treats the local cache as the single source of truth and uses Kotlin Flow plus WorkManager to deliver offline-first reliability for FDA-regulated medical IoT and BLE wearable apps.

insights.claim: Reactive Data Layer Architecture treats the local database as the single source of truth, uses the network only to populate it, and exposes cold Kotlin Flow streams so the UI subscribes rather than queries one-shot.
insights.speaker: Mervyn Anthony
insights.so_what: A reusable architecture for offline-first reactive apps; relevant for engineering teams building connected-hardware mobile products.
insights.locator: Reactive Data Layer Architecture (RDLA) is a pattern

insights.claim: Classic CLEAN Architecture imposes a 'boilerplate tax' of trivial pass-through Use Case classes for simple reads and offers no guidance on local-remote sync or SQLite limits, which RDLA eliminates by letting the presentation layer observe repository streams directly.
insights.speaker: Mervyn Anthony
insights.so_what: Argues established enterprise patterns are misaligned with mobile constraints, justifying a specialized data-layer approach.

insights.claim: BLE device apps suffer the 'GATT race condition' where the Bluetooth controller processes commands out of order, producing poorly documented GATT Status 133 or 129 errors, which RDLA mitigates by serializing hardware operations via suspendCancellableCoroutine bridges.
insights.speaker: Mervyn Anthony
insights.so_what: Identifies a concrete, common failure mode in connected medical/wearable hardware that an architecture pattern can address.

insights.claim: For offline-first writes, RDLA queues user mutations locally for immediate optimistic UI updates and delegates background cloud sync to Android Jetpack WorkManager, which resumes from the last memory offset despite Doze mode or process death.
insights.speaker: Mervyn Anthony
insights.so_what: Offloading durability to the OS service layer is the key to guaranteeing critical payload delivery in regulated apps.
insights.locator: For standard logs (like recording heart rate BPM

insights.claim: On remote rejection (HTTP 409 Conflict or 422 Unprocessable Entity), the worker flags the mutation FAILED instead of infinite-retrying, purges it from Room, and the reactive merged flow plus distinctUntilChanged automatically reverts the UI to the last server-confirmed state.
insights.speaker: Mervyn Anthony
insights.so_what: Demonstrates graceful conflict-resolution and rollback without corrupting the local source of truth, important for clinical data integrity.
insights.locator: Optimistic local updates provide a seamless user experience

insights.claim: RDLA draws on patterns the author developed building FDA-cleared Android data architectures for Bose Corporation, including dual-node sleep wearables and self-fitting hearing aids.
insights.speaker: Mervyn Anthony
insights.so_what: Signals the pattern is battle-tested in shipping regulated consumer medical hardware, increasing its credibility for similar products.
